description:
You are a Linux systems engineer reviewing hardening configurations.  
A pre-existing SQLite database file is available at:

/home/user/configs/security_audit.db

That database already contains a table named “services” with three columns:
 • id      INTEGER  
 • name    TEXT  
 • status  TEXT    (holds the literal strings “enabled” or “disabled”)

Goal: generate a CSV report that lists only the services whose status is “enabled”.

Required final artefact:
1. Path          : /home/user/output/enabled_services.csv  
2. File format   : plain text CSV produced by sqlite3 with “-header -csv” options.  
3. Exact content :  
   Line-1 (header) : id,name  
   Subsequent lines: one line per enabled service, sorted by ascending id, with fields separated by a literal comma “,”, no surrounding quotes or extra spaces.  
   A single UNIX newline (“\n”) must appear at the end of every line, including the last.

Example of the fully correct file (DO NOT hard-code—query the database):

id,name
1,sshd
2,cron
4,ufw

Constraints & hints:
• Achieve the entire extraction in exactly one sqlite3 CLI invocation piped/redirected to create the CSV file.  
• If /home/user/output/ does not yet exist, create it first with standard user permissions.  
• Do NOT modify the database; only read from it.  
• The automated test will verify:  
  – The file /home/user/output/enabled_services.csv exists.  
  – Its content matches the specification byte-for-byte (including newlines, header text, order, and absence of quotes/spaces).  

Once the file is created as specified, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial environment (pre-task):
1. Directory /home/user/configs exists.
2. SQLite database /home/user/configs/security_audit.db exists and contains:

   CREATE TABLE services (
       id     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
       name   TEXT NOT NULL,
       status TEXT NOT NULL
   );

   INSERT INTO services (id, name, status) VALUES
       (1, 'sshd', 'enabled'),
       (2, 'cron', 'enabled'),
       (3, 'cups', 'disabled'),
       (4, 'ufw',  'enabled');

3. Directory /home/user/output does NOT exist by default.

Expected artefact after correct execution:
Path: /home/user/output/enabled_services.csv  
Permissions: -rw-r--r-- (default umask 022) owned by user.  
Contents (including final newline):

id,name\n
1,sshd\n
2,cron\n
4,ufw\n

(Where “\n” represents a single UNIX newline character.)

Automated test will:
• Confirm /home/user/output/enabled_services.csv exists.  
• Read its bytes and compare exactly to:

id,name\n1,sshd\n2,cron\n4,ufw\n

Case, commas, order, and newlines must match exactly.
name: task_000000_530d88ed
